description.summary: Skinned Tom is a creature from Appalachian folklore described as a humanoid figure completely devoid of skin, exposing raw red muscle, sinew, and tissue beneath.
description.full: Skinned Tom is a creature from Appalachian folklore described as a humanoid figure completely devoid of skin, exposing raw red muscle, sinew, and tissue beneath. Standing roughly human height but with an unsettling gait, this entity is said to inhabit the deep hollows and remote mountain regions of the Appalachian Mountains. While its appearance is universally described as terrifying, accounts of its behavior vary—some portray it as a malevolent entity that stalks travelers, while others suggest it is more curious or even pitiful, possibly cursed or suffering. The legend has roots in oral tradition passed down through generations of mountain communities, with few documented modern sightings but persistent presence in regional folklore. Skinned Tom represents a distinctive entry in Appalachian folklore, reflecting the region's rich tradition of cautionary tales and mysterious phenomena associated with the deep woods and isolated hollows of the mountains.

physical.morphology: See README.md for detailed physical description
